mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% suggests room for easing if inflation moderates
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2% is near threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows averages below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 726283M and HOUST at 1321K show moderate levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals in out-of-sample Rank IC comparison.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 103.5 signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 103.5 for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.9867 is above threshold but near critical level
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines below 103.5 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in payroll employment and a rise in the unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50K month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159539K and UNRATE at 4.2% show stable labor market
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or interaction signal underperforms single-series signals in Rank IC comparison over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 4.5% combined with rising consumer price inflation signals monetary tightening that increases real borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 322.132 show moderate inflation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or joint signal fails to produce higher Rank IC than single-series FEDFUNDS signal in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23685.287B (as of 4 months prior) requires latest quarterly data
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 1200K units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as construction and related sectors contract.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1200K for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1321K is above threshold but shows vulnerability to rate sensitivity
regime_hint: housing market downturn or monetary tightening impact
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following HOUST declines below 1200K are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
